name: Mr. Agha Ali Haidar
bio: Mr Agha Ali Haider son of Mr Agha Ghulam Haider was born on September 7, 1971 in Lahore. He obtained his early education from Aitchison College, Lahore and graduated in 1991 from Government College, Lahore. A politician and an agriculturist, who remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07 and 2018-23 and served as Parliamentary Secretary for Culture & Youth Affairs. He has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the third term in General Elections 2024. He has extensively travelled abroad. His father, served as Member Punjab Assembly during 1990-93 and 1997-99; and his uncle, Syed Ali Abbas remained Member of Punjab Assembly during 1997-99 and 2002-07.

name: Ahmad Iqbal Chaudhary
bio: Ahmad Iqbal Chaudhary was born on February 3, 1985. He has extensive experience in the private sector, development sector, and government.

name: Mr. Ahmer Bhatti
bio: Ahmer Bhatti has been a member of the Assembly since 2024. He is a committed and dedicated legislator and has played critical role in various pieces of legislation since becoming a member of the House. He holds the distinct honor of having served in the esteemed Civil Service of Pakistan. Moreover, he also has to his credit an illustrious career in the private sector. He has worked in two of the pioneering start ups of Pakistan which trailblazed the foreign funding scene in Pakistan. His journey into public service finds its roots in a family legacy steeped in dedication and commitment to the nation. Following in the footsteps of his esteemed father, Abdul Rasheed Bhatti, a revered parliamentarian in his own right who served twice as member of the Provincial Assembly. Bhatti's ascent to prominence is marked by a profound sense of duty and service to the people.

name: Mr. Akhtar Ali Khan
bio: Mr Akhtar Ali Khan son of Ch Abdul Wakeel Khan was born on April 1, 1957. He graduated in 1979 from Government College, Gujranwala and is a retired Government servant from Customs Department. An agriculturist, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2015-18, 2018-23 and has returned to Punjab Assembly for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2024. His brother, Ch Shamshad Ahmad Khan, served as Member, Punjab Assembly during 1992-93, 1997-99, 2002-07, 2013-15 and as Tehsil Nazim Kamoke during 2001-02 and also functioned as Minister for Transport during 2002-05 and Minister for Excise & Taxation during 2005-07. His father was also a Member of Punjab Assembly during 1988-90 and 1990-92.

name: Mr. Ali Haider Noor Khan Niazi
Mr. Ameer Muhammad Khan
bio: Mr Ameer Muhammad Khan son of Mr Aziz Ahmad Khan was born on November 17, 1972 at Bhakkar. He graduated in 1994 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. He served as Member, District Council during 1998-99; as Union Nazim, during 2000-01. An agriculturist, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2013-18 and 2018-23 and has been re-elected for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2024 He has served as Special Assistant to Chief Minister on Forest during 2018-23. He has travelled to France, Spain, Belgium, USA, UK, UAE, KSA and Holland. His father, served as Member, Punjab Assembly and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Excise & Taxation during 1988-90; as Member, National Assembly during 1990-93 and functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on Petroleum. His uncle, Mr Ahmad Hassan Khan remained Member of Punjab Assembly during 1993-96 and functioned as Advisor to Chief Minister; and his grandfather, Mr Taj Muhammad Khan served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1972-77, 1977 and 1985-88.

name: Mr. Amir Hayat Hiraj
bio: Mr Amir Hayat Hiraj son of Mehar Umar Hayat Hiraj was born on January 2, 1982. He did American High School from American School Lahore. Bachelors from GCU Lahore and BZU Multan. He served as Banker as Relationship Manager in ABN AMRO Bank during 2006-07. He was elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in 2008 and functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on Food during 2008-13. He again got elected in elections 2013. He has functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Mines & Minerals 2013-18 and functioned as Chairman Punjab Food Authority. An agriculturist, who has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the third term in general elections 2024. He has travelled to United Stated of America with the Delegation on Agriculture, United Kingdom, Germany, Austria, Romania, Saudi Arabia, Malaysia, UAE, Turkey, Nepal, Oman, Dubai Sri Lanka, Maldives, Azerbaijan and China with a Delegation for CPEC and with Delegation to Philippine on Agriculture. His uncle, Haji Khizar Hayat Hiraj served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1997-99 and Advisor to Chief Minister, Punjab 2014-18. He is a member of Standing Committees on Punjab Emergency Services, Livestock & Dairy Development and Human Rights & Minorities.

name: Mr. Arif Iqbal
bio: Rana Arif Iqbal Harnah son of Rana Muhammad Iqbal Harnah was born on September 5, 1972 at Harnah Sialkot and is a Graduate. An agriculturist, who remained Chairman Union Council 37, Mashrata, Sialkot during 2015-18. He remained Member of Provincial Assembly of the Punjab from 2018-23 and has been elected MPA in General Elections 2024. He has visited USA, Saudi Arabia and UAE. His father was Member of Punjab Assembly during 2008-13 and 2013-18 for two consecutive terms. His brother, Rana Tariq Mehmood Harnah remained Member of Punjab Assembly for few days during 2008 till his death.

name: Mr. Arif Mehmood Gill
bio: Mr Arif Mehmood Gill son of Ch Mazhar Ali Gill was born on February 20, 1973 at Sumandri. He graduated in 2002 from Bahauddin Zakariya University, Multan. He served as Member, District Council Faisalabad during 1997-99; and as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07, 2013-18 and functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on Transport. An agriculturist, who has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the third term in general elections 2024. His father served as Member Punjab Assembly for four terms during 1985-88, 1988-90, 1990-93 and 1997-99 and also functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Industries & Minerals during 1990-93 and for Labour during 1997-99.

name: Ms. Azma Zahid Bokhari
bio: Ms Azma Zahid Bokhari wife of Mr Sami Ullah Khan was born on August 18, 1976 at Faisalabad. She obtained the degree of LL.B. in 2000 from Pakistan Law College, Lahore. A practicing lawyer, who served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07, 2008-13, 2013-18 and 2018-23; functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Culture & Youth Affairs during 2009-11; as Chairperson, Standing Committee on Law & Parliamentary Affairs during 2014-17; and as Parliamentary Secretary for Information & Culture during 2017-18. She has returned to this Assembly for the fifth consecutive term in General Elections 2024 against one of the seats reserved for women. She has travelled to UK, USA, Dubai, India, Holland, Spain, Qatar and China. She visited USA in 2011 as a Member of delegation of Indo-Pakistan Young Parliamentarians; Spain to attend Socialist International Conference; and India to attend "SAFMA" Conference. Her husband remained Member Punjab Assembly during 2002-07 and is a sitting MPA from PP-144.

name: Mr. Babar Hussain Abid
bio: Rana Babar Hussain Abid son of Haji Ali Muhammad was born on August 18, 1973 at Mian Channu. He graduated from Hailey College of Commerce in 1994 and obtained the degree of MBA in 1996 from Philippines School of Business Administration, Manila, Philippines. An agriculturist, who served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13, 2013-18; functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Finance, as Chairman, Punjab Land Record Authority (PLRA), as Member PAC-II and House Committee during 2013-18 and as Member during 2018-23. He has been re-elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has travelled to Philippines, Chile, USA, Kenya, Japan and UK. To his credit, he has publication-cum-MBA thesis titled "Comparative Study of Three Leading Car Companies in Philippines (Marketing Strategies)" published in 1996.

name: Mr. Bilal Akbar Khan
bio: Mr Bilal Akbar Khan son of Mr Ijaz Muhammad Khan was born on May 2, 1980 at Gujrat. He graduated in Finance in 2004 from OHIO State University, Columbus, USA. An agriculturist, who remained Member, PUnjab Assembly during 2018-23 and has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in General Elections 2024 for the second consecutive term. He is currently functioning as Minister for Transport and Mass Transit. His uncle, Chaudhry Shafaat Ahmed Khan was MNA during 1985-88; and another uncle, Lt Col (Retd) Shujait Ahmad Khan was Member of Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for three consecutive terms during 2002-18; served as Advisor to Chief Minister Punjab during 2002-07 and as Parliamentary Secretary for Consolidation of Holdings and PDMA during 2013-18.

name: Chaudhry Muhammad Tariq
bio: Ch Muhammad Tariq Gujjar son of Ch Muhammad Bakhsh Gujjar was born on July 27, 1973 and is a graduate from University of the Punjab Lahore. He served as Member, District Council Gujranwala during 2001-05. A businessman, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13 and has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second term in general elections 2024. He has visited China, India and Thailand. His father, brother and sister are serving in Local Governments in Gujranwala.

name: Chaudhry Zafar Iqbal Nagra
bio: Ch Zafar Iqbal Nagra son of Ch Ahmad Hassan Nagra was born on July 11, 1968 at Faisalabad. He graduated in 2002 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. A businessman, who served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13, 2013-18, 2018-23; functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Housing & Urban Development during 2008-13; and as Chairman, Standing Committee on Labour & Human Resource during 2013-18. He has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has visited Japan, Thailand and Malaysia.

name: Mr. CoL(R) Ghazanfar Abbas Shah
bio: Col (R) Ghazanfar Abbas Shah son of Mr Mohammad Nawaz Shah was born on June 4, 1962 at Jhang. He graduated in 1982 from Pakistan Military Academy and obtained the degree of B.Sc (Hons) in 1994 from Command & Staff College Quetta Balochistan University. He served in Pakistan Army during 1982-2005 and retired as Lieutenant Colonel. A retired Army Officer, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in General Elections 2018 and served as Parliamentary Secretary for Colonies. He has travelled to Saudi Arabia, Dubai, Singapore, Malaysia, Turkey, Azerbaijan and Thailand. His uncle, Mr Akhtar Ali Shah remained Member, Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1965-69.

name: Mr. Fida Hussain
bio: Mr Fida Hussain son of Mian Nazar Muhammad was born on January 1, 1960 at Bahawalnagar. He served as Member, Zila Council during 1991-93; as Chairman, Market Committee Bahawalnagar during 1990-93; as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13 (functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Bait-ul-Maal); and 2013-18 (functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Forestry, Wildlife & Fisheries). A landlord, who has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. His father served as Member, Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1965-69; his brother, Mian Khadim Hussain Wattoo served as Chairman, Zila Council during 1988; as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1985-88, 1988-90, 1990-93 and 2002-07; and also served as Parliamentary Secretary for Local Government & Rural Development, Agriculture, Planning & Development and Minister for Zakat & Ushr; and as Member National Assembly during 2008-13. His nephew, Mr Abdul Gaffar is a sitting MNA.

name: Mr. Ghazanfar Abbas
bio: Mr Ghazanfar Abbas Chheena son of Malik Sharaf Sher Chheena was born on December 15, 1962 at Hyderabad Thal, District Bhakkar. He obtained the degrees of M.A and that of LL.B. in 1989 and 1990 respectively from University of the Punjab, Lahore. An agriculturist and a lawyer, who remained Member, District Council Bhakkar during 1991-93; as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2013-18, 2018-23 and has returned to Punjab Assembly for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has travelled to Thailand, Kenya and Malaysia. His uncle, Malik Aman Ullah Chheena remained Chairman, District Council Mianwali during 1980-83; and as Member, Punjab Assembly during 1990-93 and also served as Advisor to Chief Minister.

name: Mr. Ghulam Murtaza
bio: Mr Ghulam Murtaza son of Mr Saeed Ahmed was born on March 6, 1977 at Haroonabad, Bahawalnagar. He graduated in Commerce in 2000 from Punjab College of Commerce, Lahore. A businessman, who was Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07 and 2013-18 and has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the third term in general elections 2024. He has visited KSA, Egypt, Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand and UAE.

name: Mr. Ghulam Raza
bio: Mr Ghulam Raza Rabaira son of Mr Ghulam Abbas was born on December 18, 1982 at Okara. He graduated in 2003 from Government College University, Lahore and obtained the degree of M.Sc (International Relations) in 2008 from Quaid-e-Azam University, Islamabad and graduated in Law in 2011. A lawyer and an agriculturist, who remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab from 2018-2023 and is re-elected as Member Punjab Assembly in General Elections 2024.. He has travelled to Thailand, Malaysia and Dubai. His brother, Mr Nadeem Abbas Rabaira remained Tehsil Nazim during 2001-05; Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13 and functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on Communication and Works; Member National Assembly during 2013-18 and is a sitting MNA. His uncle, Mr Shafqat Abbas Rabaira served as Member Punjab Assembly during 1985-88, 1990-93 and 1993-96.

name: Ms. Hameeda Mian
bio: Ms Hameeda Mian daughter of Mian Waheeduddin was born on January 4, 1976 at Mandi Bahauddin. She obtained the degree of B.Sc in 1999 from Lahore College for Women University, Lahore. She served as Nazim, Union Council No.4 during 2001-02. A businesswoman, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07, 2013-18 and served as Parliamentary Secretary for Literacy & Non-Formal Basic Education during 2003-07; and as Minister for Women Development during 2013-18. She has been elected as a Member, Punjab Assembly for the fourth term in General Elections 2024 against one of the seats reserved for women. She has visited Thailand, USA, Japan and China on official visits. Her father, Mian Waheed Uddin served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1988-90 and 1990-93.

name: Mr. Imran Khalid Butt
bio: Mr Imran Khalid Butt son of Mr Khalid Pervaiz Butt was born on July 3, 1977 at Gujranwala. He graduated in 1996 from Government College, Gujranwala. A businessman, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13, 2013-18 and 2018-23 and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Public Prosecution during 2012-13 and as Parliamentary Secretary for Industries, Commerce & Investment during 2013-18. He has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has travelled to USA, UK, France, Holland, Germany, Spain and Morocco. His brother, Mr Salman Khalid Butt has been serving as Deputy Mayor, Gujranwala since January 2017.

name: Mr. Irfan Bashir
bio: Ch Irfan Bashir son of Ch Bashir Ahmad was born on June 12, 1979 at Gujranwala. He obtained the degree of M.A. (Political Science) in 2006 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. A landlord, who served as Nazim, Union Council, Tehsil Noshehra Virkan during 2005-07 and remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13 and 2018-23. He has returned to Punjab Assembly for the third term in General Elections 2024. His father was a Member of Punjab Assembly during 1993-96; and his uncle, Ch Abdul Rehman served as Member of Punjab Assembly during 1997-99.

name: Mr. Jaffar Ali Hocha
bio: Mr Jafar Ali Hocha son of Mr Nazir Ahmed was born on September 9, 1979 at Hocha Abadi, Tandlianwala and graduated from Government Science College, Wahdat Road, Lahore. He served as Nazim, Union Council No.78 during 2004-08; and as Chairman, Market Committee Tandlianwala during 2009-13. An agriculturist, who remained Member Punjab Assembly during 2013-18, 2018-23 and has been re-elected as Member of this Assembly for the third consecutive term in general elections 2024.

name: Mr. Javed Allo Ud Din Sajid
Ms. Kanwal Pervez Chaudhry
bio: Ms Kanwal Pervaiz Ch wife of Mr Liaqat Ali was born on September 12, 1981 at Jamkay Cheema Sialkot. She obtained the degree of LL.B. in 2007 from Punjab University Law College, Lahore; and did M.A. (Political Science) in 2009 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. An Advocate, who remained Member, Punjab Assembly from 2018-2023 and has been re-elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in General Elections 2024 against one of the seats reserved for women. She is currently serving as Parliamentary Secretary for Environment Protection Department. She also served as Member, Metropolitan Corporation, Lahore during 2016-18. She was awarded Best Lady Lawyer Award for the year 2012 from Lahore Bar Association. She is a Member of Lahore High Court Bar Association; Lahore Bar Association; and Tax Bar Association.

name: Khawaja Mohammad Waseem
bio: Khawaja Muhammad Waseem son of Khawaja Aman Ullah was born on April 17, 1969 at Narowal. His academic qualification is Intermediate. He started his political career from grass root level and was elected as Councillor of the Municipal Committee Narowal. Later on he was elected as Chairman of the Municipal Committee Narowal. He remained  Tehsil Nazim Narowal for two consecutive terms. A businessman, who was Member Punjab Assembly during 2013-18 and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Cooperatives during 2017-18 and has been elected for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2018. He has visited many foreign countries including Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, Hong Kong, Germany and Dubai. His wife, Mrs Samina Waseem remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13 and served as Chairperson, Standing Committee on Special Education.

name: Khawaja Muhammad Mansha Ullah Butt
bio: Khawaja Muhammad Mansha Ullah Butt son of Khawaja Muhammad Abdullah Butt was born on June 30, 1939 at Sialkot and is a graduate. He served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for four terms during 1990-93, 1993-96, 1997-99, 2013-18 and became Member of Punjab Assembly for fifth term in General Elections 2024. He also functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Labour during 1990-93; as Political Secretary to Chief Minister during 1997-99; as Special Assistant to Chief Minister during 2009-13; and as Minister for Local Government & Community Development during 2016-18. A businessman, who has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the fifth term in General Elections 2018. He visited China and Turkey in 2017 on official visit as a Minister.

name: Mr. Khayal Ahmad
bio: Mr Khayal Ahmad Kastro son of Mr Muhammad Iqbal was born on November 18, 1971 at Faisalabad and is a graduate. A businessman, who remained Member, Punjab Assembly during 2018-23 and has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has served as Minister for Colonies with additional charge of Culture Department during 2018-23. He has travelled to UK and USA. His brother-in-law, Dr Muhammad Shafique Chaudhry served as Member, National Assembly during 1985-88; as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1990-93, 1993-96, 2002-07 and functioned as Minister for Excise & Taxation and Literacy & Non-formal Basic Education during 2003-07.

name: Malik Ahmad Saeed Khan
bio: Malik Ahmed Saeed Khan son of Mr Muhammad Hanif Khan was born on March 23, 1975 at Kasur. He obtained the degree of LL.B. in 2000 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. An agriculturist, who remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07 and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Consolidation and during 2013-18 & 2018-23. He has been elected as Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the fourth term in General Elections 2024. He belongs to a well-known political family. His uncle, Haji Sardar Khan remained Member, Punjab Assembly for three consecutive terms during 1985-93; his another uncle, Malik Rasheed Ahmad Khan remained Member of Punjab Assembly during 1997-99 and also functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Social Welfare; as MNA during 2012-13, 2013-18 and is a sitting MNA. His cousin, Malik Muhammad Ahmad remained Member Punjab Assembly during 2002-07 (functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Parliamentary Affairs), 2013-18, 2018-23 and 2024-till date and is a serving as Speaker, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab.

name: Malik Muhammad Ahmad Khan
bio: Malik Muhammad Ahmad Khan stands as a distinguished figure in national politics and law, boasting a rich tapestry of experience spanning over two decades. As the incumbent Speaker of the Assembly, he brings a wealth of knowledge and expertise to his role. Hailing from district Kasur, Mr. Khan has dutifully represented his constituency in the Assembly for an impressive fourth consecutive term, a testament to the unwavering trust and confidence of his constituents. His journey into public service finds its roots in a family legacy steeped in dedication and commitment to the nation. Following in the footsteps of his esteemed father, Malik Muhammad Ali, a revered parliamentarian in his own right who served as both a member of the Provincial Assembly and as the Deputy Chairman of the Senate, Khan's ascent to prominence is marked by a profound sense of duty and service to the people.

name: Malik Zaheer Iqbal Channer
bio: Malik Zaheer Iqbal Channer son of Malik Muhammad Iqbal Channer was born on August 30, 1983 at Bahawalpur. He graduated in 2005 from Sadiq Egerton College, Bahawalpur. An agriculturist, a landlord and a politician, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab from 2018-23 and has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2024 and is functioning as Deputy Speaker, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab. His father remained Member, Punjab Assembly for three consecutive terms during 2002-18; and functioned as Minister for Special Education and Prisons during 2008-13; as Minister for Cooperatives during 2013-18. His brother, Malik Munir Iqbal Channer remained Deputy Mayor, Bahawalpur.

name: Mr. Mannan Khan
bio: Rana Mannan Khan son of Ch Maqbool Ahmed was born on October 1, 1976 at Sialkot and graduated in 1996 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. A businessman, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2013-18 and has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2018. Being a Member of Punjab Assembly he attended National Security Workshop at National Defence University, Islamabad. He has travelled to UK, Turkey, Saudi Arabia, China, UAE and Thailand.

name: Mehar Muhammad Nawaz
bio: Mehr Muhammad Nawaz son of Mehr Muhammad Fuzail was born on October 11, 1963 at Mauza Satiana, Jhang. He graduated from Government College, Jhang. An agriculturist, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the fifth term in bye-elections held on July 17, 2022 against the seat vacated by Mehar Muhammad Aslam Bharwana, who was de-notified on 23 May, 2022 by the Election Commission of Pakistan in terms of Article 63A(1)(b)(i) of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. He made an oath as a Member Punjab Assembly on 21 July 2022. He remained Member, District Council Jhang in 1987; and also functioned as Chairman, Accountability Board, District Jhang during 1991-92. He served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for four consecutive terms during 1988-99 and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Consolidation during 1988-90; as Parliamentary Secretary for Physical & Environmental Planning; as Advisor to Chief Minister for two times during 1993-96. His father, served as Member, Zila Council, Jhang. His uncle, Mehar Ghulam Haider Bharwana remained Member Punjab Legislative Assembly during 1951-55; Member Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1956-58; and as Member National Assembly during 1962-64, 1972-77 and 77. His cousin, Ms Saleem Bibi is a sitting MPA from PP-96.

name: Ms. Mehwish Sultana
bio: Ms. Mehwish Sultana daughter of Sultan Raja Azmat Hayat was born on August 30, 1980 in Chakwal. After completing her graduation, she obtained an MBA degree from the University of Arid Agriculture, Rawalpindi in 2003, and later completed a one-year Diploma in French Language from the National University of Modern Languages (NUML), Islamabad in 2005.

name: Mian Muhammad Shoaib Awaisi
bio: Mian Muhammad Shoaib Awaisi son of Mian Shahab-ud-Din Awaisi was born on August 6, 1971 at Bahawalpur and is an intermediate. He started his political career as a Chairman, Market Committee Bahawalpur in 1996 and served as a Member, District Council Bahawalpur during 1998-99. He was elected as Nazim, Union Council No.29 Khanqua Sharif in 2001 and served as such till 2005. He served as Tehsil Nazim Sadar Bahawalpur during 2005-09; and as Administrator, Market Committee Bahawalpur during 2003-05. He remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2013-18 and functioned as Chairman Parks & Horticulture Authority Bahawalpur during 2014-18. A landlord and a businessman, who has been elected as Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2018. He has travelled to Saudi Arabia, Malaysia, UAE, Thailand and Singapore. He is a descendant of a well-known Saint of Sub-continent, Hazrat Khawaja Mohkum Din Serani and hails from a well-known political family of Bahawalpur. His grandfather, Mian Muhammad Qasim Awaisi served as Member, Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1956-58; his father, Mian Shahab-ud-Din Awaisi, remained Member Punjab Assembly for four consecutive terms during 1985-88, 1988-90, 1990-93, 1993-96 and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Housing, Physical & Environmental Planning, Planning & Development and Auqaf. His maternal uncle Mian Sardar Ahmad Awaisi was Member of Punjab Assembly during 1972-77 and 1977; his paternal uncle, Mian Najeeb-ud-Din Awaisi served as Member, Punjab Assembly during 1997-99; as Tehsil Nazim, Bahawalpur during 2000-04; as Member National Assembly during 2013-18 and is a sitting MNA. His another paternal uncle, Mian Saif-Ullah-Awaisi was Member of Punjab Assembly during 2002-07.

name: Mian Yawar Zaman
bio: Mian Yawar Zaman son of Mian Muhammad Zaman was born on March 21, 1961 at Okara. He got his early education from Sadiq Public School, Bahawalpur and graduated in 1980 from Government College, Okara. An agriculturist, who remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07, 2008-13 (functioned as Member of Public Accounts Committee); and 2013-16 (Minister for Irrigation) 2016-18 (Minister for Forest, Wildlife & Fisheries). He has returned to the Assembly for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2018. He has extensively travelled abroad. He belongs to a well-known political family. His grandfather, Mian Ghulam Muhammad was Member of Punjab Legislative Assembly during 1951-55; and his father was Member of National Assembly during 1985-88, 1990-93, 1997-99 and also functioned as Minister of State; as Federal Minister; and as Chairman, Federal Land Commission.

name: Mr. Mohammad Kashif
bio: Mr Muhammad Kashif son of Mahr Rang Elahi was born on January 27, 1976 at Sheikhupura. He obtained the degrees of B.Sc(Hons) (Economics) in 2000 from University of London (Lead College LSE), London, UK; and M.Sc (Economic Development and Planning) in 2006 from UCL, London, UK. An agriculturist and an academic manager, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2013-18 and has returned to this Assembly for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has visited Canada, UK, Europe, Dubai, Africa and Middle East. His father, served as Member, District Council during 1979-83.

name: Mr. Muhammad Aamir Inayat Shahani
bio: Mr Aamir Inayat Khan Shahani son of Mr Inayat Ullah Khan was born on December 1, 1983 at Bhakkar. He graduated in 2003 from Government Degree College, Bhakkar. An agriculturist, who remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2013-18, 2018-23 and has been re-elected for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on Planning & Development during 2018-23. He has visited Thailand, India, Malaysia, Sri Lanka and Singapore. He belongs to a prominent political family. His uncle, Mr Aman Ullah Khan Shahani served as Member, Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1962-65, 1965-69; as Member, Punjab Assembly during 1977; and as Member National Assembly during 1985-88. His cousin, Mr Naeem Ullah Khan Shahani remained Member, Punjab Assembly during 1990-93 (served as Advisor to Chief Minister), 1997-99 and 2002-07 (Minister for Sports during 2003-05); and his another cousin, Mr Aleem Ullah Khan Shahani functioned as Tehsil Nazim during 2001-05. His father, served as District Naib Nazim during 2001-05; his brother, Mr Asif Inayat Khan Shahani was Chairman, Union Council during 2015.

name: Mr. Muhammad Ali Raza Khan Khakwani
Mr. Muhammad Ayub Khan
bio: Lt Col (R) Sardar Muhammad Ayub Khan son of Sardar Muhammad Murad Khan was born on March 14, 1961 at Toba Tek Singh. After obtaining initial education from Government College University, Lahore, he was commissioned in Pakistan Army and graduated from Pakistan Military Academy, Kakul in 1980. He also obtained the degree of LL.B. in 2005 from Punjab Law College, Lahore. He retired from the Army as Lieutenant Colonel in 2002. A practicing lawyer and an agriculturist, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13 and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Environment and S&GAD. He was again elected in 2013 and 2018. He has also functioned as Minister for Counter Terrorism during 2017-18. He has returned to Punjab Assembly for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He officially visited China in 2016. His father, served as Member of Punjab Assembly for four consecutive terms during 1985-88 (also functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Communication & Works), 1988-90 (also remained Special Assistant to Chief Minister), 1990-93 (Advisor to Chief Minister) and 1993-95; and his brother, Sardar Muhammad Masood Khan remained Member, Punjab Assembly during 1995-96, 1997-99 (also functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Auqaf, Zakat & Ushr).

name: Mr. Muhammad Ilyas
bio: Maulana Muhammad Ilyas Chinioti son of Maulana Manzoor Ahmad Chinioti was born on December 31, 1961 at Chiniot. In addition to several Islamic courses, he obtained the degree of M.A (Arabic) in 1983 from Wifaq-ul-Madaris Al-Arabia, Multan and M.A (Islamiyat) in 2008 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. He earned a Diploma in Arabic Language from Ummul Qurra University, Makkah, Saudi Arabia in 1986. He served as Ameer, International Khatam-e-Nabuwat Movement Pakistan in 2004. A teacher, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13, 2013-18, 2018-23 and has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. A book titled "The Case of Hazrat Essa in the Court of Christians" published in 2011 and a book titled "Arbaeen-e-Khatam-e-Nabuwat" published in 2014 are also to his credit. He has travelled to Indonesia, Uzbekistan, Japan, Mozambique, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, United Arab Emirates, Bangladesh, Malaysia, Hong Kong, Thailand and India. His father remained Chairman, Tehsil Municipal Committee Chiniot during 1992-94; and served as Member, Punjab Assembly for three terms during 1985-88, 1988-90 and 1997-99.

name: Mr. Muhammad Latif Nazar
bio: Mr Muhammad Latif Nazar son of Mr Nazar Muhammad was born on January 15, 1966 at Faisalabad. He graduated in 1988 from Municipal Degree College, Faisalabad. He remained Naib Nazim for two terms during 2001-09. A businessman, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2018-23 and has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has servied as Chairman, Parks & Horticulture Authority, Faisalabad during 2018-23. He has visited Japan, Canada, USA and Saudi Arabia.

name: Mr. Muhammad Moeen Ud Din Riaz
Mr. Muhammad Naeem Safdar
bio: Mr Muhammad Naeem Safdar Ansari son of Haji Safdar Ali Ansari was born on January 26, 1982 in Lahore. He obtained the degree of M.A. (Political Science) in 2016 from University of Sargodha and passed LL.B. in 2017 from Bahauddin Zakariya University, Multan. He remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13, 2013-18, 2018-23 and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Communication & Works during 2017-18. A businessman, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has travelled to USA, Saudi Arabia, France, Switzerland and UK.

name: Mr. Muhammad Nawaz Chohan
bio: Mr Muhammad Nawaz Chohan son of Mr Muhammad Sharif Chohan was born on January 1, 1953 at Gujranwala and is a Matriculate. He remained Member, Provincial Assembly of Punjab during 2012-13, 2013-18 and served as Parliamentary Secretary for Transport and as Chairman, GDA & WASA, Gujranwala during 2013-18. A businessman and an agriculturist, who has returned to Punjab Assembly for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2018. His brother, Dr. Muhammad Ashraf Chohan remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-12.

name: Mr. Muhammad Shoaib Siddiqui
bio: Mr. Muhammad Shoaib Siddiqui son of Mr. Muhammad Idrees Siddiqui was born on May 29, 1969 in Lahore. He obtained the degrees of M.Sc. Physics in 1991 and M.Sc. Mathematics in 1992 respectively from University of the Punjab, Lahore. He has served as Member, Punjab Board of Technical Education, Lahore during 2005-2007; as Member Syndicate, King Edward Medical University during 2007; and as Member Syndicate, Government College University, Lahore during 2006-2007; and Member Syndicate, University of Engineering and Technology (UET) Lahore during 2024-2029. Two publications titled "Investigation of Gamma by Activity and Radiological Hazards" published in 1991 in Science International; and "Radiation Hazards from Building Materials" published in 1993 in Arabian Journal of Science and Engineering, UAE and Health Physics International to his credit.

name: Mr. Mumtaz Ali
bio: Mr Mumtaz Ali Khan Chaang son of Mr Matara Khan Chaang was born on July 10, 1976 at Rahim Yar Khan and is an Intermediate. A landlord and a contractor, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second term in bye-elections held on April 21, 2024. He has travelled to UK.

name: Ms. Nargis Faiz Malik
bio: Miss Nargis Faiz Malik daughter of Mr Faiz Muhammad Malik is a graduate. She served as Member, District Assembly Rawalpindi during 2001-06. She has widely travelled abroad and has visited UK, USA, France, Dubai, Belgium, Singapore, Bangladesh, Thailand, South Korea, etc. A business lady and a social worker, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in general elections 2024 against one of the seats reserved for women. She remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab from 2008-13 and served as Parliamentary Secretary for Information Technology from 31 January 2009 to 26 February 2011.

name: Ms. Nasreen Riaz
bio: Syndicate Member of Kinnaird College for Women University, Lahore. Syndicate member of Govt. Sadiq College Women University, Bahawalpur. Focal Person of Child Protection Bureau, Rahim Yar Khan.

name: Mr. Naveed Aslam Khan Lodhi
bio: Mr Naveed Aslam Khan Lodhi son of Mr Muhammad Aslam Khan Lodhi was born on December 25, 1974 in Lahore. He graduated in 1993 from Government College, Lahore and obtained the degree of LL.B. from Punjab Law College, Lahore. An advocate, who remained District Chairman, Zakat & Ushr Sahiwal during 2002-05; Nazim and Chairman Union Council Harappa District Council Sahiwal during 2005-09 and 2015-18 respectively. He remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2018-23 and has been elected as Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2024. His uncle/father-in-law, Mr Muhammad Arshad Khan Lodhi remained Member Punjab Assembly during 1972-77, 1985-88, 1988-90, 1990-93, 1997-99, 2002-07, 2013-18 and has also served as Parliamentary Secretary, as well as Minister holding various portfolios including Colonies, Revenue, Relief, Industries, Mineral Development, Transport, Agriculture, Livestock & Dairy Development, Consolidation and Planning & Development Departments. His father remained Chairman, District Council Sahiwal during 1997-99.

name: Mr. Noor ul Amin Wattoo
bio: Mr Noor-ul-Amin Wattoo son of Mian Mohammad Amin Khan Wattoo was born on October 20, 1981 at Sahiwal. He obtained the degree of B.Sc(Hons) in Computer Sciences in 2005 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. An agriculturist, who remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab from 2018-2023 and is re-elected as Member Punjab Assembly in General Elections 2024. He has travelled to Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand and Saudi Arabia. He comes from a family having seasoned and renowned parliamentarians. His uncle, Mian Muhammad Yasin Wattoo remained Member Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1962-65, 1965-69 and functioned as Minister for Education, Basic Democracies, Local Government and Social Welfare during 1965-69. He remained Member National Assembly during 1985-88 (functioned as Minister for Finance), 1990-93 (Chairman Bait-ul-Maal) 1993-96, 1997-99 (functioned as Minister for Parliamentary Affairs). His another uncle, Mr Mohammad Moeen Wattoo remained Chairman, District Council Okara during 1998-99; Member of Punjab Assembly during 2008-13; and Member National Assembly during 2013-18 and is a sitting MNA.

name: Mr. Qaiser Iqbal
bio: Mr Qaiser Iqbal Sandhu son of Ch Ghulam Mustafa was born on December 31, 1960 at Gujranwala. He obtained the degree of LL.B in 1985 from University of Sindh. He remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13, 2013-18 and 2018-23 and served as Chairman, Standing Committee on Social Welfare, Women Development & Bait-ul-Maal during 2010-13. An agriculturist, who has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the fourth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has travelled to Saudi Arabia, Dubai and Sweden. His cousin, Mr Zulfiqar Ali Bhinder served as Member, Punjab Assembly during 2008-13 and is a sitting MNA.

name: Mr. Ramesh Singh Arora
bio: at Nankana Sahib. He graduated from the Government College University. He is a leading Entrepreneur & prominent Sikh Leader who hails from Kartarpur, Narowal. A businessman, who has been elected as a Member, of the Provincial Assembly of Punjab for the third consecutive term in 2024. In his first tenure during 2013-18, he was the first Parliamentarian in the Punjab Assembly who came from the Sikh Community after the Independence of Pakistan in 1947.

name: Rana Abdul Sattar
bio: Rana Abdul Sattar Khan son of Rana Shamim Ahmad Khan was born on March 2, 1982. He served as Member, National Assembly during 2008-13; and as Member Punjab Assembly during 2013-18 and 2018-23. He has been re-elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2024. His father remained Member, Punjab Assembly for five terms during 1985-96 and 2008-13; and also served as Advisor to Chief Minister during 1993-96; as Member National Assembly during 2013-18 and 2018-23 and functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on Interior & Narcotics and is a sitting MNA.

name: Rana Aftab Ahmad Khan
bio: Mr Aftab Ahmad Khan son of Mr. Irshad Muhammad Khan was born on August 3, 1952 at Faisalabad. He obtained the degree of LL.B. in 1973 and that of M.A (Political Science) in 1975 from University of the Punjab, Lahore. He is a practising lawyer. As a delegate, he has participated in various International Conferences held in Brazil (1990), UK (1993), China, Australia, Singapur (1994), Malaysia, Thailand (1995) and as an observer, US Presidential Election, he visited USA in 1998. He served as Member Punjab Assembly during 1988-1990, 1993-96 and 2002-2007 and also functioned as Provincial Minister for Planning and Development during 1994-1995. He has returned to Punjab Assembly for the fourth term in General Elections 2024. His father was Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1977 and his uncle, Rao Muhammad Afzal Khan, was Member Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1962-65, 1965-69, Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1972-77 and 1977; and Member National Assembly during 1988-90.

name: Rana Mohammad Iqbal Khan
bio: Rana Muhammad Iqbal Khan son of Rana Phool Muhammad Khan was born on April 20, 1945 at Gumthala District Karnal, India. He obtained the degree of LL.B. from Punjab University Law College, Lahore. An advocate, an agriculturist and a seasoned/veteran politician who served as General Secretary, Tehsil Bar Association, Chunian during 1975-76; as Vice Chairman, Zila Council, Kasur during 1983-87; as Member, Provincial Council of the Punjab during 1983-84; and as Chairman, Zila Council Kasur for two consecutive terms during 1987-93. He was first elected unopposed, to the Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in bye-elections 1993, but before he could make oath as a Member, the Assembly was dissolved in June 1993. He returned to the Assembly in 1993 and served as Member, Punjab Assembly during 1993-96. He was re-elected to the Punjab Assembly for the third term in 1997 and served as Provincial Minister holding various portfolios during 1997-99. In the fourth and fifth term, he functioned as Speaker Punjab Assembly during 2008-18. He remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2018-23 and functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on Housing, Urban Development & Public Health Engineering. He has been re-elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the seventh term in General Elections 2024. He has extensively travelled abroad on both official and personal visits. He belongs to a notable and acclaimed family of politicians. His father, Rana Phool Muhammad Khan was Member of Punjab Assembly for six consecutive terms during 1965-69, 1972-77, 1977, 1985-88, 1988-90 and 1990-93, and also served as provincial minister during 1988-90 and 1990-93. His cousin, Rana Muhammad Hayat Khan remained Member National Assembly for three terms during 1990-93, 1997-99 and 2013-18. His another cousin, Rana Muhammad Ishaq Khan was a Member of National Assembly during 2008-13, 2013-18 and is a sitting MNA.

name: Rana Muhammad Saleem
bio: Rana Muhammad Saleem son of Rana Muhammad Hanif was born on April 16, 1961 at Khanewal and is a Matriculate. He served as Nazim, Union Council Mubarakpur Kabirwala for two consecutive terms during 2001-08. A businessman, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in General Election 2024. He remained Member, Punjab Assembly from 2021-23.

name: Rana Munawar Hussain
bio: Rana Munawar Hussain alias Rana Munawar Ghous Khan son of Rana Muhammad Ghous was born on April 1, 1972 at Sargodha. He graduated in 1991 from Government College, Sargodha. He served as Tehsil Nazim, Sillanwali during 2001-05; as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13, 2013-18; and as Member, Public Accounts Committee during 2017-18. An agriculturist, who has returned to the Punjab Assembly for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2018. He has travelled to Japan, UAE and Saudi Arabia. He attended a Commonwealth Workshop in Johannesburg, South Africa in 2017. His father, Rana Muhammad Ghous Khan was a famous political leader of Sargodha.

name: Rao Kashif Raheem Khan
bio: Rao Kashif Rahim Khan son of Rao Abdul Rahim Khan was born on March 1, 1970 at Samundri. He obtained the degrees of M.A. in 1996 and that of LL.B. in 2001 from Punjab University Law College, Lahore. An agriculturist and a lawyer, who served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13, 2013-18 and has been re-elected as Member, Punjab Assembly for the third term in general elections 2024.

name: Ms. Riffat Abbasi
bio: Ms. Riffat Abbasi is an active female politician known for her dedication and grassroots activism. She is serving as a Member of the Provincial Assembly of the Punjab from PML-N. Her strong commitment to empowering women and advocating for their rights has been a driving force in her work.

name: Mr. Saeed Akbar Khan
bio: Mr Saeed Akbar Khan son of Mr Ghulam Akbar Khan was born on June 8, 1955 at Bhakkar. He graduated from University of the Punjab, Lahore. He remained Member Punjab Assembly during 1985-90 and had successively been elected during 1993-2013. An agriculturist, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the eighth term in General Elections 2024. He functioned as Parliamentary Secretary during 1985; as Advisor to Chief Minister during 1986-88; as Minister for Livestock & Dairy Development during 1988-90; Minister for Communication & Works during 1993-96; and as Minister for Prisons during 2003-07. His brother, Mr Rasheed Akbar Khan remained Member National Assembly during 1988-90, 1993-96, 2002-07 and 2008-13.

name: Mr. Sardar Abdul Aziz Khan Drishak
Sardar Alhaj Ghulam Ahmad Khan Gadi
Sardar Muhammad Awais Dreshak
bio: Sardar Muhammad Awais Dreshak son of Sardar Ghulam Mujtaba Dreshak was born on December 18, 1972 at Rajanpur. After completing Intermediate in 1991 from Aitchison College, Lahore, he graduated in 1993 from Government College University, Lahore. He obtained the degree of M.Sc. (International Relations) in 1996 from Quaid-e-Azam University, Islamabad. An agriculturist, who has been elected as Member, Punjab Assembly uncontested in terms of Section 75 of the Elections Act, 2017 (XXXIII of 2017), and took oath on October 4, 2018. Because the returned candidate in the said constituency died immediately after General Elections held on July 25, 2018. He worked with United Nations for more than a decade in various fileds like Governance, Health, Education and Advocacy. He belongs to a family of well-known and seasoned politicians, who have roots in political arena of Pakistan even before partition. His grandfather, Sardar Allan Khan Dreshak was Member of Punjab Legislative Council during 1921-23; and another grandfather, Sardar Bahadur Khan Dreshak remained Member of Punjab Legislative Council during 1930-36; Member of Punjab Legislative Assembly during 1946-47, 1951-55; Member of West Punjab Legislative Assembly during 1947-49; and Member of Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1956-58. His father was Member of Punjab Assembly during 1990-93; and served as Chairman, District Council Rajanpur during 1983-87. His uncle, Sardar Nasrullah Khan Dreshak served as Member, Punjab Assembly for six terms during 1972-93, 2013-18 and functioned as Minister for Irrigation & Power, Law & Parliamentary Affairs and Planning & Development. He was also a Member of National Assembly during 1997-99, 2002-07 and is a sitting MNA. His uncles, Sardar Ramzan Khan Dreshak and Sardar Karam Elahi Khan Dreshak were Members of Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1962-65 and 1965-69 respectively. His cousin, Dr Hafeez Ur Rehman Khan Dreshak was MNA during 2013-18; and another cousin, Sardar Amanullah Khan Dreshak served as Member, Punjab Assembly for four consecutive terms during 1993-13 and functioned as Advisor to CM during 1995-96. His three cousins namely Sardar Hasnain Bahadar Dreshak (Minister for Livestock & Dairy Development), Sardar Ahmad Ali Khan Dreshak and Sardar Farooq Amanullah Dreshak are sitting MPAs. His cousin, Sardar Ali Razza Khan Dreshak was Member of Punjab Assembly during 2013-18 and remained District Nazim Rajanupr during 2005-08; and another cousin, Sardar Khalil ur Rehman Dreshak served as Tehsil Nazim for two consecutive terms during 2001-08.

name: Mr. Sher Ali Khan
bio: Mr Sher Ali Khan son of Col Mumtaz Ali Khan was born on July 24, 1961 in Rawalpindi. He got his early education from Aitchison College, Lahore and obtained the degree of B.Sc in 1981 from FG Sir Syed College, Rawalpinidi. He is an agriculturist, who served as Member, District Council Attock during 1987-93. He remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-13 and 2013-18 and has returned to Punjab Assembly for the third term in general elections 2024. He also worked as Minister for Energy from 11-06-2013 to 12-11- 2013. He has extensively travelled abroad. He belongs to a renowned political family. His grandfather, Sardar Sir Muhammad Nawaz Khan served as Member, Central/ Punjab Legislative Assembly during 1927-49; functioned as Minister for Defence & Frontier Region during 1948-50 and as Pakistan's First Ambassador to France during 1950-52. His grandfather, Nawab Malik Amir Muhammad Khan served as Member, Punjab Legislative Assembly during 1951-55; as Member, Provincial Assembly of West Pakistan during 1956-58 and also functioned as Governor of West Pakistan during 1960- 66; his uncle, Malik Fateh Khan served as Member, Punjab Assembly during 1972-77; and his another uncle, Nawabzada Malik Muzaffar Khan remained Member, National Assembly during 1965-69, 1972-77 and 1977-77. His cousin, Malik Atta Muhammad Khan served as Member, Punjab Assembly during 1990-93 and his another cousin, Maj (R) Malik Azam Khan Gheba remained Member, Punjab Assembly during 2012-13.

name: Mr. Sohaib Ahmed Malik
bio: Malik Sohaib Ahmed Bherth son of Dr Malik Ijaz Ahmed Bherth was born in Lahore. He obtained the degrees of LL.B. (Hons.) in 2006 and LL.M (International Law) from London Metropolitan University, UK. A practicing lawyer, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab from 2018-23 and has been re-elected for the second term as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in General Elections 2024. He is serving as Minister for Communication & Works and also hold additional portfolio of Minister for Law. He has visited UK, Dubai, Thailand, Turkey, Malaysia and Saudi Arabia. His father was Member of Punjab Assembly for two terms during 1988-90 and 1993-96; and his brother, Dr. Malik Mukhtar Ahmed Bherth served as Member, Punjab Assembly for three consecutive terms during 2002-07, 2008-13 and 2013-18; and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary during 2006-07; as Political Assistant to Chief Minister Punjab; as Minister for Population welfare during 2017-18; and as Chairman, Public Accounts Committee-II during 2013-18 and is a sitting Member of National Assembly of Pakistan.

name: Mr. Sultan Bajwa
bio: Mr. Sultan Bajwa son of Tariq Mehmood Bajwa was born on 18th September in Nankana Sahib. He did his Bachelor’s in Computer Science from Brunel University, London and Master of Management from BPP University, London, UK. Sultan Bajwa did his Graduate Diploma in Law in 2021 from Leeds Beckett University, Leeds, UK and then got a Degree in Bar at Law in 2023 from Lincoln’s Inn, London, UK. A barrister and a businessman who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in General Elections 2024. His father, Mr Tariq Mehmood remained Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2008-2013 & 2013-2018.

name: Syed Ejaz Hussain Bukhari
bio: Syed Ejaz Hussain Bukhari son of Syed Ghulam Murtaza Bukhari was born on March 5, 1941 at Attock. He obtained the degree of M.A in 1966 and that of LL.B in 1967 from University of Karachi, Karachi. A practicing lawyer, who remained President, District Bar Attock during 1980-82 and served as Chairman, District Council Attock during 1983-87 and as Opposition Leader, District Council during 1997-99. He remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07, 2013-18 and has returned to Punjab Assembly for the third term in general elections 2024. He has travelled to Iran, Iraq, Syria and Egypt.

name: Ms. Tahseen Fawad
bio: Mrs Tahseen Fawad wife of Shaikh Fawad-ur-Rasheed was born on November 13, 1959 in Lahore. She served as Tehsil Member, Rawal Town during 2005-08; as Member, Bait-ul-Maal District Rawalpindi during 2011-13. She has served various NGOs. She was Executive Member of Aurat Foundation during 1998; served as Member, CPDI during 2003; and is serving as President, Dia Welfare Foundation from 2013. A Social worker, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in general elections 2013 against one of the seats reserved for women.

name: Mr. Taimoor Ali Lali
bio: Mr Taimoor Ali Lali son of Mr Amjad Ali Lali was born on August 6, 1989 at Lalian Chiniot. He obtained the degree of LL.B. in 2016 from Bahauddin Zakariya University, Multan. An agriculturist, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2018-23 and has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has served as Parliamentary Secretary for Auqaf & Religious Affairs during 2018-23. He belongs to a renowned political family. His uncle, Mr Imtiaz Ahmed Lali served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1997-99, 2002-07 and 2013-18; his another uncle, Mr Masood Ahmed Lali was Member of Punjab Assembly during 2008-13; and his another uncle, Mr Ghulam Muhammad Lali was Member of National Assembly during 2013-18 and is a sitting MNA.

name: Mr. Tanveer Aslam Malik
bio: Mr Tanveer Aslam Malik son of Malik Muhammad Aslam was born on June 10, 1972 in Lahore. He graduated in Commerce in 1992 from University of Karachi and obtained the degree of MBA (Marketing) in 1995 from South Eastern University, Washington DC, USA. A businessman, who served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2002-07, 2008-13, 2013-18 and 2018-23; functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Services & General Administration Department during 2003-05; as Minister for Housing, Urban Development & Public Health Engineering during 2013-16; and as Minister for Communication & Works during 2013-18. He has returned to Punjab Assembly for the fifth consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He has visited USA, UK, Malaysia, UAE, Thailand, Sweden, UN, Turkey, Ethiopia, China, Australia, Hong Kong, Japan and Saudi Arabia.

name: Mr. Tariq Subhani
bio: Ch Tariq Subhani son of Ch Akhtar Ali was born on December 1, 1971 at Sialkot. He obtained Diploma from Nutley High School, New Jersey, USA and graduated in Business Administration in 1997 from William Patterson University, Patterson, USA. He has extensively travelled abroad. A businessman, who has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab in general elections 2024. He remained MPA Punjab Assembly from 2013-18. His father remained Member Punjab Assembly during 1985- 88 (functioned as Special Assistant to Chief Minister) and 1988-90 (served as Minister for Communication & Works); as Member, National Assembly during 1993-96; and also functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on Narcotics. His brother, Ch Khush Akhtar served as Member, Punjab Assembly during 1990- 93, 1993-96 and 2002-07; and functioned as Chairman, Standing Committee on food during 1990-93 and Minister for Population Welfare and Prisons, Housing & Physical Planning, Environmental Planning during 1993-96.

name: Mr. Zahid Akram
bio: Mr Zahid Akram son of Haji Muhammad Akram was born on October 21, 1982 at Chishtian and is a Matriculate. An agriculturist, who served as Member Punjab Assembly during 2013-18 and 2018-23. He has functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Colonies during 2018-23. He has been re-elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the third consecutive term in General Elections 2024. His father remained Member Punjab Assembly during 1985-88, 1988-90, 1990-93, 1997-99; and Tehsil Nazim, Chishtian during 2005-09.

name: Ms. Zakia Khan
bio: Begum Zakia Khan wife of Mr Shahnawaz Khan Niazi was born on March 5, 1946 at Isa Khel. She is a matriculate and studied at St. Denys School Murree and Queen Mary College, Lahore. During the General Elections held in 1964, she campaigned with Ms. Fatima Jinnah for revival of democracy in Pakistan and thus started her political career. She served as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 1977, 1985-88, functioned as Advisor to Chief Minister during 2009-13 and performed the duties of Minister for Population Welfare during 2013-18. A house wife and a politician, who has returned to Punjab Assembly for the fifth term in General Elections 2024 against one of the seats reserved for women and is functioning as Chairperson, Standing Committee on Environment Protection. She served as Member Senate Punjab University during 1986; and as Member, Board of Management Committee Services Hospital Lahore during 2013. She is serving as Member, Executive Committee Punjab Council of Arts; as Member, Executive Committee Punjab Halal-e-Ahmer; and as Member, Khidmat Committee District Mianwali. She represented Pakistan in United Nations on 47th Session of the Commission on Population and Development at New York, USA during 2014. She has travelled to Europe, USA, Canada, North Africa, Middle East, Kashmir, Afghanistan and India. Her father-in-law, Col (Retd) Muhammad Aslam Khan Niazi was Member of Punjab Assembly during 1972-77 and 1977. Her three brothers have served as Member Provincial Assembly of the Punjab; Mr Abdul Hafeez Niazi during 2008-13 and functioned as Parliamentary Secretary for Zakat, Ushr and Bait-ul-Mal; Mr Abdul Razzaq Khan Niazi during 1985-88 (and functioned as Chairman, Standing Committees on Information and S&GAD), 2002-07, 2013-18; and Mr Abdul Rehman Khan during 1993-96; functioned as Minister for Prison during 1995-96 and is a sitting MPA from PP-85 for the second term.

name: Mr. Zeeshan Rafique
bio: Mr Zeeshan Rafique son of Mr A. D. Rafique was born on June 30, 1980 at Sialkot. He obtained the degree of M.Sc (Computer Science) in 2003. A businessman, who remained Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab during 2018-23 and has been elected as Member, Provincial Assembly of the Punjab for the second consecutive term in General Elections 2024. He is currently functioning as Minister for Local Government & Community Development. He has visited UK, Turkey, France, Belgium, Germany, UAE, Oman, Holland and USA.
